text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of code without changing how it works. The main goal is to make the code easier to read, understand, and maintain. For example, you might break a long function into smaller ones or give variables clearer names. Refactoring does not add new features or fix bugs; it simply makes the code cleaner. Over time, messy code accumulates what developers call "technical debt." This debt slows down future work because it becomes harder to make changes safely. Regular refactoring helps pay off this debt, keeping the codebase healthy and efficient.
